text:
Ceremonial vs. Culinary Matcha: What Beginners Should Know

Matcha is often sold as either "ceremonial grade" or "culinary grade,"

but these labels are marketing terms and are not officially regulated in Japan. That means

the same powder could be labeled differently by two different brands.

In general, ceremonial-style matcha is smoother and less bitter, and is meant to be

whisked with just hot water and enjoyed plain. Culinary-style matcha is stronger and more

t to be

whisked with just hot water and enjoyed plain. Culinary-style matcha is stronger and more

bitter, which makes it useful for lattes, baking, and ice cream where milk and sugar balance

the flavor.

For a beginner ordering at a cafe, the practical takeaway is simple: if you want something

smooth to sip on its own, look for a ceremonial-style drink; if you want a sweeter milk-based

drink, a latte made with culinary matcha is perfectly fine and usually cheaper. Do not over-trust

drink, a latte made with culinary matcha is perfectly fine and usually cheaper. Do not over-trust

the grade label alone — taste and sweetness level matter more for whether you will enjoy it.

Molly Tea — Matcha Menu

Matcha Latte 'classic' & smooth. Stone-ground matcha shaken with fresh milk.

Recommended sweetness: 50%. A great starting point for beginners who find straight

matcha too bitter, because the milk softens the grassy edge.

Cloud Matcha — our matcha latte topped with a layer of salted cheese foam.

The salted cheese foam adds a creamy, slightly savory top layer that balances the

bitterness of the matcha. Many first-timers say this is the easiest matcha drink to enjoy.

es the

bitterness of the matcha. Many first-timers say this is the easiest matcha drink to enjoy.

Jasmine Matcha: matcha blended with jasmine green tea. Lighter and more floral,

with a thinner body than the latte. Best with oat milk if you want it creamier.

r/MatchaEverything • Posted by u/teabeginner

I might get roasted for this but I think HEYTEA's matcha is actually great for beginners.

Top comment (312 upvotes): Honestly agree. The Cloud Matcha is sweet and creamy and

doesn't taste bitter at all. If you're new to matcha and scared of the grassy

flavor, start there. Rating: 4.5/5.

Reply: Disagree a little — for me the salted cheese foam was too much. I prefer the

ating: 4.5/5.

Reply: Disagree a little — for me the salted cheese foam was too much. I prefer the

plain matcha latte with oat milk and 30% sugar. Still beginner friendly though, just less sweet.

Reply: For a STRONGER matcha taste, ask for less sugar and skip the fruit toppings.

The fruit versions bury the matcha flavor completely.
